mnemonics: HIC-A: A Group must have (H) identity, (I) inverses, (C) closure, and (A) associativity.
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that satisfies closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
The identity element is unique in a group.
The inverse of any element is unique in a group.
commonMistakes: Assuming commutativity (ab = ba); groups are not necessarily abelian.
Forgetting to check the closure property for a subset when proving it's a subgroup.
Misapplying the cancellation law (ab = ac implies b = c) in structures that are not groups.
